sentence: Subjective:
HPI: This 20-year-old female was seen today for a yearly follow-up for asthma, allergic rhinitis, and food allergy.
She reports that she has been tolerating her current medication regimen well, taking Wixela 250/50 twice a day and Zyrtec as needed for seasonal allergy symptoms.
She has a known peanut allergy but tolerates almonds and cashews.
She has been trying to reduce her use of her albuterol inhaler, taking it only for strenuous activities.
She has a history of vaping and smoking but is currently trying to quit.
Past medical history: Known peanut allergy, asthma, allergic rhinitis, seasonal allergies.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Wixela 250/50, twice a day
Zyrtec, as needed
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's ears had a little bit of wax on both sides, and her lungs sounded terrific.
Her breathing test results are about the same as they were a couple of years ago and look a little bit better.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's asthma is well-controlled, and she is tolerating her current medication regimen well.
Problem list: Asthma; Allergic rhinitis; Food allergy; Peanut allergy.
Plan:
I will call in a new prescription for Alvesco 80 mcg, two puffs twice a day, and instruct the patient to continue using her albuterol inhaler as needed.
I will also renew her epipen and Zyrtec prescription.
I will instruct the patient to continue to stay away from peanuts and tree nuts.
Follow-up: The patient will return in four months for a follow-up appointment to repeat her breathing test and assess her asthma control.
